findings.message: Multi-step body (check active → read state → remove files → report) presented in unnumbered prose; the four sequential steps have no explicit numbering.

Empty $ARGUMENTS not validated: running /review-loop with no argument silently creates a state file with a blank task body and starts the loop. The standalone setup-review-loop.sh correctly exits with an error for empty input but the command's inline bash omits this check.
Multi-step command body (setup → implement → stop → run Codex → address feedback) presented in unnumbered prose paragraphs rather than a numbered list, making sequencing ambiguous.
Command declares argument-hint but the body never specifies behavior when $ARGUMENTS is blank. Duplicate of Bug #1; counted as quality penalty too.
No output format defined. The command ends with behavioral RULES but provides no report template or completion summary structure for what Claude should say when the review loop finishes.
Vague quantifier: 'thoroughly and completely' has no measurable criteria.
Vague quantifier: 'well-structured' has no measurable criteria.
Vague quantifier: 'well-tested' has no measurable criteria.
Install instruction 'npm install -g @openai/codex' has no version pin. A breaking change in the Codex CLI could silently break the review workflow without any error at install time.

REVIEW_LOOP_CODEX_FLAGS is read from the environment and expanded unquoted at line 350 into a heredoc-generated shell script. If the variable contains shell metacharacters (;, &&, |), they are baked into .claude/review-loop-run-codex.sh and execute when Claude runs bash on it.

The UX agent section of build_review_prompt() instructs the Codex agent to run 'npm install -g agent-browser' with no version pin. If the package name is squatted or typosquatted, a Codex agent operating on a UI project will install a malicious package.

Script header uses ERR trap for cleanup but omits 'set -u' and 'set -o pipefail'. Unbound variables silently produce empty strings; pipeline failures in the middle of a compound command may go undetected.

scripts/setup-review-loop.sh is a fully-implemented setup script with proper dependency and empty-input checks, but commands/review-loop.md embeds equivalent logic inline and never calls the script. The script has no documented callers and may drift from the inline bash over time.

Empty $ARGUMENTS not validated: running /review-loop with no argument silently creates a state file with a blank task body and starts the loop. The standalone setup-review-loop.sh correctly exits with an error for empty input but the command's inline bash omits this check.
Multi-step command body (setup → implement → stop → run Codex → address feedback) presented in unnumbered prose paragraphs rather than a numbered list, making sequencing ambiguous.
Command declares argument-hint but the body never specifies behavior when $ARGUMENTS is blank. Duplicate of Bug #1; counted as quality penalty too.
No output format defined. The command ends with behavioral RULES but provides no report template or completion summary structure for what Claude should say when the review loop finishes.
Vague quantifier: 'thoroughly and completely' has no measurable criteria.
Vague quantifier: 'well-structured' has no measurable criteria.
Vague quantifier: 'well-tested' has no measurable criteria.
Multi-step body (check active → read state → remove files → report) presented in unnumbered prose; the four sequential steps have no explicit numbering.

REVIEW_LOOP_CODEX_FLAGS is read from the environment and expanded unquoted at line 350 into a heredoc-generated shell script. If the variable contains shell metacharacters (;, &&, |), they are baked into .claude/review-loop-run-codex.sh and execute when Claude runs bash on it.

The UX agent section of build_review_prompt() instructs the Codex agent to run 'npm install -g agent-browser' with no version pin. If the package name is squatted or typosquatted, a Codex agent operating on a UI project will install a malicious package.

Script header uses ERR trap for cleanup but omits 'set -u' and 'set -o pipefail'. Unbound variables silently produce empty strings; pipeline failures in the middle of a compound command may go undetected.
Install instruction 'npm install -g @openai/codex' has no version pin. A breaking change in the Codex CLI could silently break the review workflow without any error at install time.

scripts/setup-review-loop.sh is a fully-implemented setup script with proper dependency and empty-input checks, but commands/review-loop.md embeds equivalent logic inline and never calls the script. The script has no documented callers and may drift from the inline bash over time.
